extras.title: Half-size breadboard
extras.subtitle: 400 tie-point solderless prototyping board
extras.description: The backbone of any hobby build. Snap it next to the Mini Proto Unit or mount it on the I/O Hub to wire circuits without soldering.

extras.title: Dupont jumper wires
extras.subtitle: Male-Male + Male-Female + Female-Female assortment
extras.description: Colour-coded silicone wires with 2.54 mm Dupont ends. Bridge anything to anything, fast. The MF/FF variants are what you need for the HY2.0-4P breakouts.

extras.title: 5 mm LED assortment
extras.subtitle: Through-hole LEDs, red / yellow / green / blue / white
extras.description: The cheapest and most satisfying output you'll ever wire. Always pair with a ~220 Ω current-limiting resistor.

extras.title: Resistor starter pack
extras.subtitle: 1/4 W carbon-film, 220 Ω / 1 kΩ / 10 kΩ and friends
extras.description: You will burn through these. Useful everywhere: LED series resistors, pull-ups, voltage dividers for analog sensors, base resistors for transistors.

extras.title: Tactile pushbuttons
extras.subtitle: 12 mm momentary switches, breadboard-friendly
extras.description: Satisfying clicky buttons for arcade games, morse keys, menus, or anything that needs user input beyond the single device button.

extras.title: Active piezo buzzer
extras.subtitle: 5 V active buzzer, ~2 kHz tone
extras.description: Drop-in audio alerts. Drive it directly from a GPIO pin for beeps, alarms and morse. Passive piezos also work — you'll just need to toggle them at the desired pitch.

extras.title: Photoresistor (LDR)
extras.subtitle: GL5528 ~10 kΩ light-dependent resistor
extras.description: A cheaper, simpler alternative to the Light Sensor Unit when you only need a coarse ambient-light read. Pair with a 10 kΩ resistor in a divider.

extras.title: NTC thermistor
extras.subtitle: 10 kΩ @ 25 °C NTC bead
extras.description: The cheapest temperature sensor you'll find. Apply the Steinhart–Hart equation (or a small lookup table) to turn the raw ADC reading into °C.

extras.title: 3–6 V hobby DC motor
extras.subtitle: Small 130-size brushed motor
extras.description: Add motion to any project: fans, shakers, vibrating puzzle bases. Always drive through a transistor and a flyback diode — never directly from a GPIO pin.

extras.title: NPN transistor + flyback diode
extras.subtitle: 2N2222 / BC547 + 1N4007
extras.description: The classic combo for switching small motors, solenoids and relays from a microcontroller GPIO. The diode protects the MCU from the back-EMF kickback.

extras.title: 8 Ω mini speaker
extras.subtitle: 0.5 W 28 mm speaker
extras.description: Richer audio than a piezo — good for theremins, tone sequencers and retro game sound effects. Drive it via PWM with a ~100 Ω series resistor.

extras.title: Reed switch + magnet
extras.subtitle: Glass-encapsulated reed + neodymium magnet pair
extras.description: Closes when a magnet passes — rock-solid door/window sensor, rotation counter and bike-speedo staple. No contact wear because the switch is sealed.

extras.title: Ball-bearing tilt switch
extras.subtitle: SW-520D mercury-free tilt sensor
extras.description: A tiny metal capsule with a ball that bridges two contacts when tilted. Instant anti-theft or fall-detection sensor with no code required beyond a read.

extras.title: Rotary potentiometer
extras.subtitle: 10 kΩ linear pot + matching knob
extras.description: A classic analog dial — volume knob, brightness knob, colour wheel. Works with any ADC-capable GPIO via a simple 3-wire hookup.

extras.title: SPDT microswitch
extras.subtitle: Lever-actuated limit / endstop switch
extras.description: Lever-arm microswitch, perfect as an endstop for moving parts, a coin sorter gate, or a tamper switch under anything hinged.
